name: Nukus
description: Nukus is the capital of Karakalpakstan on the Amu Darya — administrative, economic and cultural centre since 1932. Population 330,000+ (2024 ~343,500). Known for the Savitsky art museum and the desert setting near the Kyzylkum. Founded on the site of ancient Shurcha fortress; awarded Friendship Order 2003. Savitsky Museum holds the world’s second-largest collection of Russian avant-garde; regional museum and Mizdakhan cemetery are key sights.

monuments.title: Savitsky State Art Museum
monuments.info: Founded 1966 by Igor Savitsky — “Louvre in the Sands”; major avant-garde collection and Karakalpak folk art.

monuments.title: Mizdakhan complex
monuments.info: Archaeological cemetery and town from 4th c. BC to 14th c. AD near Khojeyli over 200 ha — “clocks of the world” legends; mausoleums and underground mosque.

monuments.title: Chilpyq (Sun minaret)
monuments.info: 1st c. BC–4th c. AD Zoroastrian tower of silence on a hill south of Nukus by the Amu Darya; linked to solar rites.

monuments.title: Sultan Uvays Qarani complex
monuments.info: 12th–19th c. shrine in Beruniy District to early Islamic saint Uvays al-Qarani; mosque, chillakhana and sacred pool with “holy fish”.

monuments.title: Topraq Qala
monuments.info: 2nd–3rd c. AD Khorezmian fortress-palace near Nukus, UNESCO-listed; wall paintings and rich finds from ancient Khorezm.
